body: assertTrue("www.apache.org IP should be valid", validator.isValid("140.211.11.130"));
assertTrue("www.l.google.com IP should be valid", validator.isValid("72.14.253.103"));
assertTrue("fsf.org IP should be valid", validator.isValid("199.232.41.5"));
assertTrue("appscs.ign.com IP should be valid", validator.isValid("216.35.123.87"));
assertTrue(String.format("%s should be valid", item), validator.isValid(item));
assertFalse(String.format("%s should be invalid", item), validator.isValid(item));
assertTrue("class A IP should be valid", validator.isValid("24.25.231.12"));
assertFalse("illegal class A IP should be invalid", validator.isValid("2.41.32.324"));
assertTrue("class B IP should be valid", validator.isValid("135.14.44.12"));
assertFalse("illegal class B IP should be invalid", validator.isValid("154.123.441.123"));
assertTrue("class C IP should be valid", validator.isValid("213.25.224.32"));
assertFalse("illegal class C IP should be invalid", validator.isValid("201.543.23.11"));
assertTrue("class D IP should be valid", validator.isValid("229.35.159.6"));
assertFalse("illegal class D IP should be invalid", validator.isValid("231.54.11.987"));
assertTrue("class E IP should be valid", validator.isValid("248.85.24.92"));
assertFalse("illegal class E IP should be invalid", validator.isValid("250.21.323.48"));
translation: self.assertTrue("class A IP should be valid", self.__validator.isValid("24.25.231.12"))
self.assertFalse("illegal class A IP should be invalid", self.__validator.isValid("2.41.32.324"))
self.assertTrue("class B IP should be valid", self.__validator.isValid("135.14.44.12"))
self.assertFalse("illegal class B IP should be invalid", self.__validator.isValid("154.123.441.123"))
self.assertTrue("class C IP should be valid", self.__validator.isValid("213.25.224.32"))
self.assertFalse("illegal class C IP should be invalid", self.__validator.isValid("201.543.23.11"))
self.assertTrue("class D IP should be valid", self.__validator.isValid("229.35.159.6"))
self.assertFalse("illegal class D IP should be invalid", self.__validator.isValid("231.54.11.987"))
self.assertTrue("class E IP should be valid", self.__validator.isValid("248.85.24.92"))
self.assertFalse("illegal class E IP should be invalid", self.__validator.isValid("250.21.323.48"))

body: assertTrue("localhost IP should be valid", validator.isValid("127.0.0.1"));
assertTrue("broadcast IP should be valid", validator.isValid("255.255.255.255"));
assertFalse("IP with characters should be invalid", validator.isValid("124.14.32.abc"));
assertFalse("IP with leading zeroes should be invalid", validator.isValid("124.14.32.01"));
assertFalse("IP with three groups should be invalid", validator.isValid("23.64.12"));
assertFalse("IP with five groups should be invalid", validator.isValid("26.34.23.77.234"));
translation: self.assertFalse(self.__validator.isValid("124.14.32.01"), "IP with leading zeroes should be invalid")
self.assertFalse(self.__validator.isValid("23.64.12"), "IP with three groups should be invalid")
self.assertFalse(self.__validator.isValid("26.34.23.77.234"), "IP with five groups should be invalid")
self.assertFalse(self.__validator.isValidInet6Address(""), "IP empty string should be invalid")  # empty string

body: assertTrue("IPV6 fe80:: should be valid", validator.isValidInet6Address("fe80::"));
"::ffff:192.0.2.128")); // but this is OK, since there's a single digit
assertFalse("IPV6 ldkfj should be invalid", validator.isValidInet6Address("ldkfj"));
"::0:a:b:c:d:e:f")); // syntactically correct, but bad form (::0:... could
